body.title: day-close creates new-day slate on date rollover
body.result: Added Step 1 to day-close SKILL.md: ensure current-day slate exists (create from template if missing). Renumbered steps 3-13 to 4-14 and updated hard-rules. Created 20260424.slate.txt to unblock current day.
body.description:
day-close typically runs at the start of the next day, after midnight rollover. It closes yesterday's slate — but nothing in the skill guarantees today's slate exists. If context-load is skipped (as happened 2026-04-24: /day-close errored 'Prompt is too long' at session start and work pivoted elsewhere), today never gets a slate and every later 'cat today's slate' step silently fails.

Fix: add a Step 1 to day-close — ensure current-day slate exists; create from the context-load template if missing. Rule: every day has a slate.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/day-close/SKILL.md
**Design rules:** none apply (skill file is markdown procedure).
**Scope boundary:** IN — day-close SKILL.md edit. OUT — context-load changes, slate_manager changes, session_manager changes.
**Test plan:** no tests because: skill is markdown procedure, not executable code. Verified by creating today's slate manually as part of this /fixit.
